description: Fix WARN-level ABC audit issues: add suite README files, populate sdlc_phase, document difficulty rationale, and regenerate selected_benchmark_tasks.json.

userStories.title: Add README.md to suites missing benchmark descriptions
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want every suite to have a README.md describing what it measures, so that R.3 passes for all suites.
acceptanceCriteria: Every benchmarks/csb_*/ directory has a README.md file
Each README.md contains: one-paragraph description, what the suite measures, task count, difficulty level rationale
python3 scripts/abc_audit.py --all --format table 2>&1 | grep R.3 shows no WARN entries
userStories.notes: Check which suites already have README.md. Only create for those missing. Content should be brief (5-10 lines). Suite descriptions can be derived from the suite name and sample task.toml descriptions.

userStories.title: Regenerate selected_benchmark_tasks.json from task.toml files
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want selected_benchmark_tasks.json to be a complete manifest of all canonical tasks, so that T.7 and R.4 checks can work.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json contains an entry for every task.toml in benchmarks/csb_*/ (excluding backups/templates)
Each entry has: task_id, benchmark (suite name), repo, language, difficulty, sdlc_phase
Language values use normalized canonical forms (go, java, cpp, python, typescript, rust, csharp, javascript)

python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json')); tasks=d.get('tasks',d) if isinstance(d,dict) else d; print(len(tasks if isinstance(tasks,list) else list(tasks.values())))" prints >= 404
userStories.notes: Check if scripts/sync_metadata.py or similar exists. If not, write a script that reads all task.toml files and generates the JSON. Map suite names to sdlc_phase: csb_sdlc_* suites map to their suffix (debug, design, etc.), csb_org_* suites get sdlc_phase='org' or a more specific mapping. Use parse_task_toml_simple from abc_audit.py or a proper TOML parser.

userStories.title: Document difficulty distribution rationale
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the difficulty distribution documented with rationale for why 94% hard is intentional, so that R.9 resolves.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/TASK_SELECTION.md (or appropriate doc) contains a section explaining the difficulty distribution
The section explains that tasks are intentionally hard/expert because the benchmark targets enterprise-scale scenarios
The section includes the actual distribution counts (hard: ~392, expert: ~21, medium: ~3)
userStories.notes: Check if docs/TASK_SELECTION.md already exists. If so, add a section. If not, create it. Keep it brief.
